Cheryl J. creen
Executive Director

Shernan Oaks, CA 91403
Dear I'Is .

behalf of

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. A-91-3I3
Green :
l-etter is in response to your request for advice oh
Telephone Answering Services of California Inc..
the campaign provisions of the Political 
Reforn Act (the
OUESTIONS
I. 
May funds deposited in the organization,s sponsored
political 
action committee account be transferred to the
organization's general operation account upon termination of the
committee?
2. What are the procedures for terninating a political
action comrnittee?
CONCLUSIONS
1. 
Funds deposited in the committee's account nay not be
transferred to the organization's general operation account.
2. 
PoIiticaI action comrnittees which neet the criteria
described in the fotlowing analysis may terminate their filing
obligations by filing a Statement of Termination (Form 415),.
1/ covernment code Sections 810o0-91,0L5. All statutory references
are to the Governnent code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations Section
18OOo, et seq. AII references to regulatj.ons are to TitLe 2,
Divj,sion 6 of the California Code of Regul,ations.
428 J StrcCt,Suitc 800 ● P.0.Bo、807 ● Sacrantcnto CA 95804´ 0807 0 (916)322‐ 5660


Cheryl J. creen
Page 2
FACTS
The Telephone Answering Services of California Inc. sponsors
the TASC-PAC (Telephone Answering Services of cal,ifornia p;Iitical
Action comnittee), a conmittee formed in 1980. The committee
rrants to terminate and transfer approximately $7,OOO of conmittee
funds into the organization,s general operation account.
ANALYSIS
Effective January 1, 1990, the Act placed statutory
restrictj-ons on the use of campaign funds. (Sections 80510-
89521.) canpaign funds include any contributions, cash, cash
equivalents and other assets received or possessed by a sponsored
cornmittee. GeneraIIy speaking, any expenditure of clmpaign funds
must be, at a minimum, reasonably related to a politi6ll,
Iegislative, or lrovernmental purpose. (Section g9512.) A
transfer of funds by a sponsored committee to tfre sponsorrs
general- operation account does not appear to serve i political,,
governmental, or legislative purpose. ( Brovrn Advice Letter, No.
I-9O-412, copy enclosed. )
A committee rnay terminate its f iJ.ing obligations if aI1 of
the following criteria are net:
(a) The comnittee has ceased to receive contributions and
make expenditures;
(b) The conmittee does not anticipate recej_ving
contributions, repa)rments of outstanding loans made !o others, or
any other receipts in the future and does not anticipate making
expenditures in the future;
(c) The committee has elininated or has dectared that it has
no intention or ability to discharge all of its debts, Ioans
r:eceived and other obligations i 
.- ,
(d) The committee has no surplus funds; and
(e) The committee has filed all of jjs required carnpaign
statements disclosing al-1 reportable transactions.
Regulation l-8404.


Cheryl J. Green
Page 3
If all- of the above criteria for terninating a recipient
cornmittee are met, the conmittee nay terminate by filinq a form
4l-5 (Statenent of Termination) with a final campaign statement,
Forn 420 or 45O.
If you have additional questions concerning this rnatter,
please contact ne at 916/322-5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
